raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing for a billing dispute regarding the incorrect application of a payment processed on XX/XX/XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I processed a payment of {$1200.00} with a customer service representative. This payment was specifically authorized and clearly intended to pay off the full promotional balance associated with a washer and dryer purchase for best buy. 
At the time of the transaction, my total account balance was {$1400.00}, which included both promotional and non-promotional charges. Upon reviewing my statement, I discovered that the payment was not applied as directed, resulting in material inaccuracies. 
As a result of the improper payment application : A promotional balance of {$460.00} remains, which should have been fully satisfied My account reflects a total balance of {$1100.00}, which is inaccurate Additional figures have appeared that are not supported by proper accounting Had the payment been applied correctly, the promotional balance would have been paid in full. From XXXX through XXXX, my correct balance owed is {$780.00}, representing only my remaining non-promotional charges, including : A minimum payment obligation of {$29.00} which is now reflecting a higher minimum of {$42.00} because of the incorrect balance. 
Additional charges totaling {$190.00} Other non-promotional purchases comprising the remaining balance I do not owe the amounts currently reflected as {$1100.00} or {$1200.00}, as these balances are the result of the payment being misapplied. I started this journey of getting this corrected XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX.
